zh.Little Walsingham, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可蕾托 沃爾辛厄姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Walsingham I於1261年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可蕾托 沃爾辛厄姆時，他的父親Geoffrey de Walsingham24歲，母親Ermitruda Isabella Walkelyn21歲。他於1282年與Christina of Walsingham在英國英格蘭諾福爾可蕾托 沃爾辛厄姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Walsingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Great or Little Walsingham in Norfolk. The placename derives from the Old English personal name Wæls + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas (genitive -inga-) + hām ‘village, homestead’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Walsingham I was born in 1261, in Little Walsingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Geoffrey de Walsingham, was 24 and his mother, Ermitruda Isabella Walkelyn, was 21. He married Christina of Walsingham in 1282, in Little Walsingham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Walsingham I est né en 1261, à Little Walsingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Geoffrey de Walsingham, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Ermitruda Isabella Walkelyn, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Christina of Walsingham en 1282, à Little Walsingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Walsingham I(이)가 1261년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 리틀 월징함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey de Walsingham(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Ermitruda Isabella Walkelyn(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1282년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 리틀 월징함에서 Christina of Walsingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.
